serious_open_bugs: #1260 — Three.js/WebGL content missing in rendered video (v0.6.x regression). Preview works, render produces black/transparent output. Maintainer (miguel-heygen) assigned within 1.5h, actively reproducing and collecting diagnostics as of 2026-06-08. Impact: any composition using Three.js/WebGL backgrounds is broken in v0.6.x output; workaround is downgrading to v0.5.5.

response_quality.first_response_time: median < 2 hours; many issues get first human response within minutes
response_quality.maintainer_engagement: high — miguel-heygen is the primary responder, consistently assigns, reproduces, and cross-references PRs
response_quality.community_health: active community contributors (sarichan777, kiyeonjeon21, abhaybhargav, ahosker, jrusso1020) provide detailed repro steps, doctor output, and debugging collaboration
response_quality.closure_discipline: strong — bugs closed with PR cross-references, duplicates identified and closed within minutes, feature requests closed when implemented
response_quality.label_usage: moderate — 'bug' and 'enhancement' labels used consistently; some issues (e.g. #1252, #1231, #1193) lack labels; only one 'good first issue' tag observed
response_quality.stale_issues: none significant — only 4 open issues, oldest from 2026-04-19 (~7 weeks); Renovate bot dashboard (#418) is intentionally perpetual

notes: Issue volume is healthy (124 closed, 4 open). The repo handles a high rate of rendering bugs (platform-specific, hardware-specific edge cases) with impressive turnaround — most critical bugs are closed same-day. The open bug #1260 (Three.js regression) is the most impactful open item and is receiving active attention. The #1231 regression had 33 comments of collaborative debugging between maintainer and reporter, exemplifying good issue triage culture. Feature requests have longer lifecycles (2-7 weeks) and are batch-closed when features ship. No evidence of issue neglect or abandonment.
